skill.name: rct-bias-assessment-rob
skill.description: Automates Risk of Bias 2 (ROB2) assessment for RCT papers by analyzing text against specific domains and synthesizing a report. Use when you need to assess the quality of a clinical trial paper or evaluate risk of bias.
skill.summary: Automates Risk of Bias 2 (ROB2) assessment for RCT papers using the Cochrane-recommended framework. Evaluates five domains and produces a structured JSON report with overall risk judgement.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 42 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate medical research tool for automated ROB2 bias assessment in randomized controlled trials. It uses Python scripts for PDF text extraction and JSON output generation. The detected patterns (markdown code formatting flagged as shell execution, plain text flagged as weak cryptography, documentation URLs flagged as network threats) are all safe, documented functionality. No evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown code formatting flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: False positive. Backtick-wrapped text in SKILL.md (e.g., `scripts/assess_rob2.py`, bash code blocks) is markdown syntax for inline code and code fences, not shell command execution. This is standard documentation formatting for a Python-based data processing skill.

low_findings.title: Text string flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: False positive. The scanner matched text containing letters common in cryptographic algorithm names (e.g., 'MD' appears in natural language text, audit file prose, and medical guideline headings). No actual MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic algorithm usage exists in any file.

low_findings.title: Natural language text flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: False positive. The scanner matched benign text strings in audit result JSON, medical guidelines, and markdown documentation. Terms like 'baseline differences suggest a problem' (ROB2 domain question) and audit notes are not system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Documentation source URL flagged as network threat
low_findings.description: False positive. The URL at SKILL.md:7 is a documentation reference linking to the legitimate GitHub source repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is a citation, not a network request executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: File write in PDF extraction script
low_findings.description: False positive. The `open(output_file, 'w')` call in scripts/extract_pdf.py:53 is the intended core functionality of a PDF text extraction tool. File path comes from argparse arguments or is auto-generated from the PDF filename. This is documented, expected behavior for a data processing skill.

content.user_title: Assess Risk of Bias in Randomized Controlled Trials
content.value_statement: Systematic reviewers and meta-analysts spend hours manually evaluating RCT quality against the Cochrane ROB2 criteria. This skill automates the assessment across five domains and produces a structured JSON report with an overall risk judgement.
actual_capabilities: Evaluate RCT papers across all 5 ROB2 domains: Randomization, Deviations, Missing Data, Measurement, and Reported Result
Extract text from PDF files for automated full-text screening and bias assessment
Generate structured JSON output with domain-level and overall risk judgements
Apply Cochrane ROB2 guidelines with documented decision rules for each domain
Produce reproducible, reviewable audit reports with explicit assumptions
Clean and validate JSON output from LLM assessments using helper scripts
limitations: Cannot assess non-RCT study designs such as cohort, case-control, or cross-sectional studies
Assessment quality depends on the completeness and clarity of the provided RCT paper text
PDF extraction quality varies for scanned documents or papers with complex formatting
Does not perform statistical meta-analysis or effect size calculation

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Quality Assessment
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a systematic review of 50+ RCTs uses this skill to evaluate each study against the Cochrane ROB2 criteria. The skill produces consistent, structured bias assessments that can be compared across studies.

use_cases.title: Peer Review of Clinical Trial Manuscripts
use_cases.description: A journal peer reviewer receives an RCT manuscript and needs to assess methodological quality. They provide the full text and receive a domain-by-domain bias evaluation with supporting evidence from the paper.
use_cases.target_user: Academic peer reviewers and journal editors

use_cases.title: Medical Education and Research Training
use_cases.description: A medical student or junior researcher learning evidence-based medicine uses this skill to practice ROB2 assessment. The structured output helps them understand how domain-level judgements combine into an overall risk score.

prompt_templates.title: Basic RCT Bias Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a Risk of Bias 2 (ROB2) assessment on the following RCT paper. Evaluate each domain: Randomization Process, Deviations from Intended Interventions, Missing Outcome Data, Measurement of the Outcome, and Selection of the Reported Result. Provide a judgement of Low Risk, Some Concerns, or High Risk for each domain and an overall risk judgement.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has the full text of an RCT paper and needs a quick bias assessment

prompt_templates.title: PDF-Based RCT Assessment with Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an RCT paper saved as a PDF file at [file_path]. First, use the extract_pdf.py script to extract the full text. Then perform a complete ROB2 assessment on the extracted text. Provide a domain-by-domain evaluation and an overall risk judgement in JSON format.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a PDF file of an RCT paper and needs end-to-end text extraction plus assessment

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Bias Assessment of Multiple RCTs
prompt_templates.prompt: I have three RCT papers on the same intervention. For each study, perform a full ROB2 assessment covering all 5 domains and the overall risk. Present the results in a single table comparing the three studies side by side, highlighting which domains drive the differences in overall risk judgement.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to compare bias across multiple studies in a systematic review

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Domain-Level Audit with Evidence Tracing
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a ROB2 assessment on this RCT paper. For each of the 5 domains, provide: (1) the risk judgement, (2) the specific text from the paper that supports your judgement, (3) the guideline criteria from the rob2_guidelines.md reference that you applied, and (4) any uncertainties or assumptions in your assessment. Flag any domains where the paper lacks sufficient information for a confident judgement.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced systematic reviewers requiring full audit trail

output_examples.input: Assess the risk of bias in this RCT: 'Smith et al. (2020) conducted a double-blind randomized trial of drug X versus placebo. Patients were randomly assigned using a computer-generated sequence. Allocation was concealed via sequentially numbered opaque envelopes...'
output_examples.output: The ROB2 assessment produced a Low Risk of Bias overall. All five domains were rated Low Risk. Domain 1 (Randomization) passed because the paper reported computer-generated sequences and opaque envelope concealment. Domain 2 (Deviations) passed with double-blinding confirmed. The structured JSON report shows each domain judgement with supporting evidence.

output_examples.input: Run ROB2 on this paper PDF and show me the output: /papers/jones_2019_rct.pdf
output_examples.output: The PDF text was extracted successfully. The ROB2 assessment found Some Concerns overall due to unclear blinding procedures in Domain 2. The paper did not describe whether outcome assessors were blinded. The remaining 4 domains were Low Risk. A JSON report was generated and saved with the full assessment including citations to specific paper sections.

output_examples.input: Compare the bias risk between study A (Chen 2021) and study B (Patel 2021) on the same treatment.
output_examples.output: Study A (Chen 2021): Low Risk overall - all 5 domains Low. Strong randomization and ITT analysis described. Study B (Patel 2021): High Risk overall - Domain 1 (Randomization) rated High due to inadequate allocation concealment using open allocation list. Domain 4 (Measurement) Some Concerns due to unblinded outcome assessment. The remaining three domains were Low Risk. A comparison table is provided.

best_practices: Provide the full text of the RCT paper including methods section for accurate domain assessment. The randomization and blinding descriptions are critical for accurate judgement.
Review the generated JSON output for each domain and verify that the supporting evidence from the paper matches the assigned risk level before using results in a publication.
Use the rob2_guidelines.md reference material to understand the decision rules for each domain before running assessments on unfamiliar study designs.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-randomized study designs such as observational studies, cohort studies, or case-control studies. The ROB2 tool is only validated for randomized trials.
Do not skip the domain-level review and jump directly to the overall judgement. Each domain must be evaluated independently to produce a valid overall risk score.
Do not modify the JSON output schema. Changes to the domain structure break compatibility with systematic review tools that consume the standardized output format.

faq.question: What is the Risk of Bias 2 (ROB2) tool?
faq.answer: ROB2 is the Cochrane-recommended tool for evaluating the risk of bias in randomized controlled trials. It assesses 5 domains: Randomization, Deviations from Intended Interventions, Missing Outcome Data, Measurement of the Outcome, and Selection of the Reported Result.

faq.question: Can this skill assess non-RCT study designs?
faq.answer: No. The ROB2 tool is specifically designed for randomized controlled trials. Other study designs such as cohort, case-control, or cross-sectional studies use different bias assessment tools like ROBINS-I or the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale.

faq.question: Does this skill extract text from PDF files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a PDF extraction script (extract_pdf.py) that uses PyPDF2 to extract text from PDF files. The extracted text is saved to a file and then analyzed using the ROB2 criteria.

faq.question: What format does the output use?
faq.answer: The output is a JSON object with fields for Study reference, each of the 5 domains (D1 through D5), and an Overall judgement. Each field contains one of three values: Low, Some concerns, or High.

faq.question: How are domain judgements combined into an overall score?
faq.answer: The overall risk follows Cochrane rules: if any domain is High, the overall is High. If no High but at least one Some concerns, the overall is Some concerns. If all domains are Low, the overall is Low.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide for a complete assessment?
faq.answer: You should provide the full text of the RCT paper including the methods section. Key details include randomization method, allocation concealment, blinding procedures, attrition rates, and outcome measurement methods.
